release: Australia release documentation on developer.servicenow.com; unversioned endpoint (latest)

params.sysparm_query: approval=approved^sys_updated_on><checkpoint>^ORDERBYsys_updated_on — strictly greater than the checkpoint, ordered so the offset scan is stable
params.sysparm_display_value: false — raw field values, not display values
params.sysparm_exclude_reference_link: true — reference fields (assignment_group) arrive as plain sys_id strings instead of {link, value} objects
params.sysparm_offset: page offset, advancing by the page size

reference_field_shapes.exclude_reference_link=false: reference fields are objects {"link": "<table api url of the referenced row>", "value": "<sys_id>"}
reference_field_shapes.exclude_reference_link=true, display_val: reference fields are plain sys_id strings
reference fields are plain display-value strings

timestamps.note: lexicographic string comparison is chronological in this format

pagination.rule: advance sysparm_offset by the page size until a page arrives shorter than the page size

checkpoint_rules.advance: only after a COMPLETE paginated scan: the new checkpoint is the max sys_updated_on among fetched records, or the old checkpoint when nothing matched
checkpoint_rules.boundary: the query uses strict '>' — a record updated exactly at the checkpoint has already been seen and must not repeat
checkpoint_rules.failure: on 401 (failure=UNAUTHORIZED), 403 (failure=FORBIDDEN) or an exhausted 429 (failure=RATE_LIMITED): return the records collected so far, complete=false, checkpoint UNCHANGED

rate_limit.rule: call the injected sleeper with the Retry-After seconds and retry the same page; after 3 failed retries give up with failure=RATE_LIMITED
